2605.28870.title: Representation Alignment Rests on Linear Structure
2605.28870.paper_summary: We investigate the Platonic Representation Hypothesis (PRH) through a tripartite statistical framework of representations: signal, bias, and noise. {1) Signal:} We propose that Platonic alignment arises from the universal relationship between objects and attributes, which is encoded linearly in representations according to the Linear Representation Hypothesis (LRH). We provide evidence that LRH helps explain PRH by extracting linear object-attribute features with sparse autoencoders and showing that these sparse representations often exhibit stronger cross-modal alignment than their dense counterparts. {2) Bias:} Models have different implicit biases due to the diverse architectures and training procedures used. We show that this difference can be partially mitigated. Centering and normalization consistently improve cross-model alignment. {3) Noise:} Finite-sample training leads to noise in representations. We provide evidence that representational noise is driven by data scarcity by revealing a strong and consistent positive correlation between word frequency and alignment in LLMs and text embedding models. Synthesizing signal, bias, and noise, we propose a statistical model that refines the Linear Representation Hypothesis and explains further phenomena related to the alignment of representations emerging from diverse modern AI architectures.
2605.28870.paper_summary_zh: 我們通過三部分的統計框架來研究柏拉圖表徵假說（PRH）：信號、偏差和噪聲。<lb/> {1) 信號：} 我們提出，柏拉圖對齊源於物體和屬性之間的普遍關係，這種關係根據線性表徵假說（LRH）在線性上編碼於表徵中。<lb/> 我們提供證據表明，LRH有助於解釋PRH，通過稀疏自編碼器提取線性物體-屬性特徵，並顯示這些稀疏表徵通常比它們的密集對應物展現出更強的跨模態對齊。<lb/> {2) 偏差：} 模型由於使用了不同的架構和訓練程序而具有不同的隱含偏差。<lb/> 我們表明，這種差異可以部分減輕。<lb/> 中心化和正規化一致地改善了跨模型對齊。<lb/> {3) 噪聲：} 有限樣本訓練導致表徵中的噪聲。<lb/> 我們提供證據表明，表徵噪聲是由數據稀缺驅動的，通過揭示詞頻與大規模語言模型（LLMs）和文本嵌入模型中的對齊之間存在強而一致的正相關。<lb/> 綜合信號、偏差和噪聲，我們提出了一個統計模型，該模型精煉了線性表徵假說，並解釋了與來自多樣化現代AI架構的表徵對齊相關的進一步現象。
2605.28870.author: Kiril Bangachev et.al.
2605.28870.authors: Kiril Bangachev, Guy Bresler, Yury Polyanskiy
